frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: neural correlates as patterns across many simultaneously recorded prefrontal neurons

frameworks.name: dimensionality reduction and decoding
frameworks.one_line: low-dimensional task-related activity patterns identified and decoded

frameworks.name: sensorimotor transformation
frameworks.one_line: mPFC linking sensory input to learned behavioral output

core_question: How does the medial prefrontal cortex transform sensory inputs into learned avoidance actions at the population level?
key_assumptions: mPFC mediates execution of learned behaviors by linking sensory to motor signals
calcium imaging population activity captures task-relevant prefrontal computation
kinematically matched spontaneous actions can serve as a control for action-specificity
manipulations: tone-signaled active avoidance learning in mice
two consecutive avoidance tasks with distinct learned actions
comparison of tone-induced vs spontaneous actions with similar motion kinematics
key_findings: large fraction of tone-evoked mPFC activity was uninformative about behavioral execution
an activity pattern was predictive of tone-induced avoidance actions
this avoidance-specific pattern did not occur for kinematically similar spontaneous actions
the avoidance-specific activity differed between two distinct learned avoidance actions
interpretations: mPFC contributes to goal-directed action selection via distributed population-level sensorimotor transformations
